title: Degrees of Separation
description:
Implement a program that determines how many degrees of separation apart two actors are using BFS (Breadth-First Search) through a movie database. Two actors are connected if they both starred in a movie together.

**Example:**
```
Name: Emma Watson
Name: Jennifer Lawrence
3 degrees of separation.
1: Emma Watson and Brendan Gleeson starred in Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix
2: Brendan Gleeson and Michael Fassbender starred in Centurion
3: Michael Fassbender and Jennifer Lawrence starred in X-Men: First Class
```

**Constraints:**
- Use BFS, not DFS (BFS guarantees shortest path)
- Track visited nodes to avoid infinite loops
- Load actor/movie data from database
- Return None if no path exists
common_mistakes: Using DFS instead of BFS - BFS guarantees the shortest path
Not tracking visited nodes, causing infinite loops in cyclic graphs
Forgetting to check if both actors exist before searching
Mutating the queue or visited set incorrectly

title: Tic-Tac-Toe with Minimax
description:
Implement an AI that plays Tic-Tac-Toe optimally using the Minimax algorithm. The AI should never lose - it should always win or draw.

**Required Functions:**
- player(board) - Returns which player's turn it is
- actions(board) - Returns set of available moves
- result(board, action) - Returns new board after move
- winner(board) - Returns winner or None
- terminal(board) - Returns True if game is over
- utility(board) - Returns +1 (X wins), -1 (O wins), or 0 (tie)
- minimax(board) - Returns optimal move using minimax algorithm

**Constraints:**
- Implement minimax recursively
- Always return a new board, never mutate the original
- Pruning is optional but recommended
common_mistakes: Not checking terminal state before recursing in minimax
Returning the wrong player (X vs O)
Mutating the board in result() instead of returning a deep copy
Incorrect utility values or winning conditions
